commands.review_summary:
Adds a profile/scenario-driven behavior eval CLI with LCM and context-engine quarantine profiles, README docs, an npm script, and focused node:test coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug report; this is a PR adding a new eval capability. The PR body does provide high-signal real behavior proof for the new local runner path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainers need to decide whether the opt-in local POC and deferred Blacksmith boundary are acceptable before merge.

target.title: Add Crabpot LCM behavior eval gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a profile/scenario-driven behavior eval POC for Crabpot so plugin categories can be tested beyond API seam compatibility.
- Add LCM as the first context-engine eval, with a default forward release gate and a historical repro profile for the recent Discord-reported break.
- The local runner installs OpenClaw and LCM into isolated temp state, configures LCM as the `contextEngine`, starts a mock-provider gateway, sends two turns, and asserts second-turn memory recall.

## HTML Artifact

- PageDrop: https://pagedrop.ai/g/Patrick-Erichsen/1218bd734f4535c9ae83552694097862
- Backing gist: https://gist.github.com/Patrick-Erichsen/1218bd734f4535c9ae83552694097862

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node --test test/behavior-eval.test.mjs` -> 11 passing tests
- `npm run eval:behavior -- --json` -> default dry plan for `forward-lcm-release-gate`
- `CRABPOT_EXECUTE_BEHAVIOR=1 npm run eval:behavior -- --execute --profile recent-lcm-2026-5-22 --runner local --timeout-ms 240000 --json` -> `status: expected-failure`, `failureClass: memory-recall-mismatch`, OpenClaw `2026.5.22 (a374c3a)`
- `CRABPOT_EXECUTE_BEHAVIOR=1 npm run eval:behavior -- --execute --profile forward-lcm-release-gate --openclaw-version 2026.5.26 --plugin npm:@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2 --expect must-pass --runner local --timeout-ms 240000 --json` -> `status: pass`, OpenClaw `2026.5.26 (10ad3aa)`
- `CRABPOT_EXECUTE_BEHAVIOR=1 npm run eval:behavior -- --execute --profile forward-lcm-release-gate --runner local --timeout-ms 240000 --json` -> `status: pass`, `openclaw@latest` resolved to `2026.5.26 (10ad3aa)`, LCM pinned to `0.11.2`
- `npm test` -> attempted after `git submodule update --init --recursive`; existing fixture/report baseline assertions still fail outside this new behavior-eval surface, including `capture-contracts`, `cold-import-readiness`, `contract-coverage`, `fixture-inspection`, `report`, `runtime-profile`, and `workspace-plan` expectations.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Crabpot can now express and execute behavioral plugin checks, starting with an LCM memory-recall release gate and a historical red repro.

Real environment tested: local macOS worktree, isolated temp OpenClaw state, npm-installed OpenClaw packages, npm-installed `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw`, loopback gateway, and mock OpenAI-compatible provider.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: see the Verification section commands above.

Evidence after fix: the historical `openclaw@2026.5.22` target fails the memory scenario with `memory-recall-mismatch`, while the same scenario passes on `openclaw@2026.5.26` and on the default `latest` forward gate.

Observed result after fix: the runner produces a classified expected failure for the red target and `pass` for both green forward targets.

What was not tested: Blacksmith remote execution. The POC records `blacksmith` as the default runner shape, but this patch executes the local runner path.

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript

<details>
<summary>Redacted Codex session transcript</summary>

```text
source: local Codex session, rendered and manually trimmed before PR insertion
redaction: raw tool outputs, local paths, auth/session details, environment details, and secrets omitted

[user]
Asked to expand Crabpot beyond API seam compatibility with categorized plugin behavior evals, starting with LCM, using Discord/Discrawl context and an HTML planning artifact.

[assistant]
Researched the recent LCM Discord chatter, narrowed the target to the last one-to-two days, and identified the recent historical repro as openclaw@2026.5.22 with @martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2.

[user]
Asked to make the POC parameterized: default forward release gate, with historical repro profiles that can be invoked by older params.

[assistant]
Planned a profile/scenario-driven Crabpot behavior eval layer: default forward LCM release gate, historical recent-lcm-2026-5-22 profile, and a runner backend that can be local now and Blacksmith-shaped later.

[user]
Asked to build it, then specifically asked whether it really failed red and passed green.

[assistant]
Built the first dry-run harness, then deepened it after the initial boundary only proved install/load. Swapped the runner to exercise gateway RPC via chat.send -> agent.wait -> chat.history, and tightened the assertion to inspect the latest assistant message instead of any transcript text.

[assistant]
Real proof found the historical pair installed and booted but failed the second-turn memory assertion with memory-recall-mismatch. The same LCM plugin passed on openclaw@2026.5.26 and on the default latest forward gate after isolating npm user config.

[user]
Asked to open a PR, include the agent transcript, and upload/include the HTML through PageDrop.

[assistant]
Created a clean branch from origin/main, uploaded the HTML artifact through the gist-backed PageDrop flow, moved only the behavior-eval changes onto the clean branch, reran the focused tests and real red/green evals, fixed a gateway teardown race, committed the patch, and prepared this PR body.

[proof]
Focused behavior-eval tests passed: 11/11.
Historical red target: openclaw@2026.5.22 + lossless-claw@0.11.2 -> expected-failure, memory-recall-mismatch.
Pinned green target: openclaw@2026.5.26 + lossless-claw@0.11.2 -> pass.
Default forward gate: openclaw@latest resolved to 2026.5.26 and LCM pinned to 0.11.2 -> pass.
Full npm test was attempted after submodules were initialized; existing fixture/report baseline assertions still fail outside this new behavior-eval surface.
```

</details>
<!-- agent-transcript:end -->
target.author: patrick-erichsen
